title: Potato Salad
cScaleSummary: C1 because the cholesterol comes from the chopped egg whites and the plain Greek yogurt. The potatoes, olive oil, dill pickle juice, celery, onion, dill pickles, carrot, fresh dill, and paprika do not contribute cholesterol, so the per-serving cholesterol remains low.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active is false because although the salad is lighter than classic deli potato salad and avoids mayonnaise and egg yolks, it is still primarily a starchy side rather than a clearly fiber-forward recipe built around core LDL-supportive ingredients like legumes, whole grains, nuts, or seeds.
intro:
American deli potato salad grew out of German and Eastern European potato salads brought to the United States in the late 1800s. Jewish and German delis in New York simplified the dish and standardized the flavor profile that most Americans now recognize: tender potatoes coated in a creamy dressing with celery, onion, and a light dusting of paprika on top. In those kitchens, mayonnaise became the dominant binder, providing both fat and acidity.

This version keeps the familiar deli flavor but rebuilds the structure without mayonnaise or egg yolks. Greek yogurt provides the creamy body, dill pickle juice supplies the deli tang, and a small portion of the potatoes are crushed during mixing to create a natural starch emulsion that thickens the dressing. Chopped egg whites restore the classic deli texture while keeping cholesterol extremely low.

items.prep: to cover potatoes by about 1 inch
soaked in cold water 5 minutes and drained

stepGroups.title: Cook the eggs
items: Place eggs in a small saucepan and cover with water. Bring to a boil, then turn off the heat and cover for 10 minutes. Transfer to cold water, peel, remove the yolks, and chop the whites. Set aside.

stepGroups.title: Cook the potatoes
items: Place the potato chunks in a pot and cover with water by about 1 inch. Add the kosher salt and bring to a gentle boil. Cook 10 to 12 minutes until the potatoes are just tender when pierced with a knife but still hold their shape.

stepGroups.title: Season the warm potatoes
items: Transfer the hot potatoes to a large mixing bowl. Immediately drizzle the 2 tbsp dill pickle juice over them and gently toss. Let them sit 5 minutes so the potatoes absorb the flavor.

stepGroups.title: Make the dressing
items: In a small bowl whisk together the Greek yogurt, olive oil, 1 tbsp pickle juice, celery seed, and black pepper until smooth.

stepGroups.title: Build the salad
items: Add the celery, onion, chopped pickles, grated carrot, and chopped egg whites to the potatoes.
Pour the dressing over the mixture.

Using a large spoon, gently fold everything together. As you mix, lightly crush a small portion of the potatoes against the side of the bowl. About 10 percent of the potatoes should break down slightly. This starch thickens the dressing and creates the classic creamy coating.

stepGroups.title: Finish with herbs
items: Fold in the chopped fresh dill.

stepGroups.title: Chill and serve
items: Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving so the dressing tightens and the flavors blend.
Transfer to a serving bowl and dust lightly with paprika just before serving.
